what_it_does: Renders a design-system preview showing three card and dropzone surface patterns: a default content card, a video tile, and an empty upload dropzone.
observations: Includes a dsCard metadata comment with group, name, subtitle, and viewport attributes.
Links to _card.css and defines file-local CSS for layout, cards, placeholders, controls, tags, and metadata text.
Uses a three-column grid inside a 700x280-oriented preview surface.
Shows a lead sentence explaining white cards, sunken media placeholders, and dashed empty states.
Uses inline styles for the gradient placeholder and for the dropzone surface variant.
standouts: Combines content, media, and empty-state card examples in one compact preview.
Uses dashed borders and a circular plus control to communicate upload affordance.
The video tile uses a sunken placeholder with a circular play control.
conventions: Uses CSS custom properties for colors, radii, shadows, and fonts.
Keeps visual examples as static HTML/CSS rather than interactive components.
Uses semantic preview text alongside visual card examples.
Mixes scoped stylesheet rules with small inline style overrides.
concerns: Inline styles make some visual choices less reusable from this file alone.
The play control uses a text glyph rather than an icon asset or accessible label.
The upload affordance is visual only and has no input element or interaction semantics.
notes: This file is a static preview specimen for card surfaces, media placeholders, and dashed upload empty states.
questions: Should the play and add controls be decorative only, or represent interactive controls?
Is the viewport metadata consumed by a preview renderer outside this file?
Are the inline style variants intentional exceptions or placeholders for tokens?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the file: it demonstrates cards, a video tile, and a dashed dropzone empty state.
intention_signals: It appears intended to document reusable card surface patterns and show how content, media, and empty upload states should look within the design system.
novelty: The embedded dsCard metadata comment gives the preview descriptive catalog information directly in the HTML file.
contribution: A fused system would keep this file's compact reference for card surfaces, sunken media placeholders, dashed upload dropzones, and the related token-driven styling cues.
summary_for_embedding: Static HTML/CSS design-system preview for default cards, video tiles, and dashed upload dropzones using tokenized colors, radii, shadows, and typography.
intention_for_embedding: This was for documenting and visually demonstrating card and empty-state surface patterns in a compact preview frame.
